truth.papers.A60.title: Enhancing the corrosion resistance of low-density steels via Al-rich oxide film formation by grain size optimization
truth.papers.A60.paper_scope: Investigating the effects of grain size on oxide film formation and corrosion behavior of Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steels.

prediction.papers.A60.title: Enhancing the corrosion resistance of low-density steels via Al-rich oxide film formation by grain size optimization
prediction.papers.A60.paper_scope: Investigate the effects of grain size and grain boundary density on Al-rich oxide film formation and corrosion behavior of Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steels prepared by solution treatment at 950–1150 °C.

truth.processes.proc_fe30mn9al09c_s0.descripti: Homogenized at 1200°C for 2 h, hot rolled at 1200–950°C to 20 mm thickness, water quenched. No subsequent solution treatment.
truth.processes.proc_fe30mn9al09c_st950.descri: Homogenized at 1200°C for 2 h, hot rolled at 1200–950°C to 20 mm thickness, water quenched; then solution treated at 950°C for 2 h and water quenched.
truth.processes.proc_fe30mn9al09c_st1050.descr: Homogenized at 1200°C for 2 h, hot rolled at 1200–950°C to 20 mm thickness, water quenched; then solution treated at 1050°C for 2 h and water quenched.
truth.processes.proc_fe30mn9al09c_st1150.descr: Homogenized at 1200°C for 2 h, hot rolled at 1200–950°C to 20 mm thickness, water quenched; then solution treated at 1150°C for 2 h and water quenched.

prediction.processes.proc_fe30mn9al09c_s0.descripti: As-hot-rolled condition without solution treatment. The ingot was homogenized at 1200 °C for 2 h, hot rolled at 1200–950 °C into 20 mm slabs, and water quenched.
prediction.processes.proc_fe30mn9al09c_s0.processes: Original sample labeled S0, featuring high residual stress, nonuniform grain structure, and retained κ-carbides.
prediction.processes.proc_fe30mn9al09c_st950.descri: Solution treated at 950 °C for 2 h with water quenching after homogenization and hot rolling.
prediction.processes.proc_fe30mn9al09c_st950.proces: Fine-grained sample labeled S950; average grain size approximately 8.7 μm with complete recrystallization.
prediction.processes.proc_fe30mn9al09c_st1050.descr: Solution treated at 1050 °C for 2 h with water quenching after homogenization and hot rolling.
prediction.processes.proc_fe30mn9al09c_st1050.proce: Intermediate grain size sample labeled S1050.
prediction.processes.proc_fe30mn9al09c_st1150.descr: Solution treated at 1150 °C for 2 h with water quenching after homogenization and hot rolling.
prediction.processes.proc_fe30mn9al09c_st1150.proce: Coarse-grained sample labeled S1150; average grain size 32.0 μm.

prediction.processing_steps.sample_s0.processing_st: Quenched in water after hot rolling.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_s950.processing_: Solution treatment followed by water quenching to room temperature.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_s1050.processing: Solution treatment followed by water quenching to room temperature.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_s1150.processing: Solution treatment followed by water quenching to room temperature.

truth.structures.sample_s0.overall_structure: Heterogeneous grain size distribution with large deformed grains and fragmented small grains; single-phase austenite (FCC); contains κ-carbides (5-10 nm) and high dislocation density.
truth.structures.sample_s0.microstructure_list: deformed and equiaxed grains with heterogeneous size
bimodal with small deformed grains and large grains
truth.structures.sample_s950.overall_structure: Recrystallized equiaxed austenite grains with abundant annealing twins (Σ3) and high-angle grain boundaries; grain size 8.7 μm; low residual stress; no precipitates.
truth.structures.sample_s950.microstructure_li: low (dislocation tangles near annealing twin boundaries)
truth.structures.sample_s1050.overall_structur: Recrystallized equiaxed austenite grains; grain size between 8.7 and 32 μm (exact value not given); reduced GB density compared to S950; no precipitates.
truth.structures.sample_s1150.overall_structur: Coarse equiaxed austenite grains, average grain size 32 μm; lowest GB density (ATBs 0.040 μm/μm^2, HAGBs 0.019 μm/μm^2); no precipitates.

prediction.structures.sample_s0.overall_structure: Single-phase FCC austenitic structure with nonuniform grain size distribution, featuring large deformed grains and fragmented small grains from thermal deformation during rolling.
prediction.structures.sample_s0.microstructure_list: nonuniform; large deformed grains with fragmented small grains at interfaces
high; irregularly distributed dislocations and dislocation tangles
nonuniform; bimodal-like with large and small grains
prediction.structures.sample_s950.overall_structure: Single-phase FCC austenitic structure with equiaxed grains and complete recrystallization, abundant annealing twin boundaries (ATBs, Σ3) and high-angle grain boundaries (HAGBs).
prediction.structures.sample_s950.microstructure_li: low; dislocation tangles adjacent to ATBs
prediction.structures.sample_s1050.overall_structur: Single-phase FCC austenitic structure with equiaxed grains and complete recrystallization; intermediate grain size between S950 and S1150.
prediction.structures.sample_s1150.overall_structur: Single-phase FCC austenitic structure with coarse equiaxed grains and complete recrystallization; reduced grain boundary density compared to finer-grained samples.

truth.interfaces.sample_s950.defect_interactio: HAGBs act as anodic sites with lowest potential, promoting Al dissolution and rapid formation of Al-rich oxide film; ATBs show higher potential and minor dissolution along boundaries.
truth.interfaces.sample_s950.phase_evolution: Fine grains and high GB density facilitate uniform and rapid formation of continuous Al-rich oxide film.
truth.interfaces.sample_s1150.defect_interacti: Low GB density leads to sparse dissolution sites, slower and discontinuous oxide film formation, and increased Fe/Mn content in film.
truth.interfaces.sample_s1150.phase_evolution: Coarse grains result in discontinuous oxide film growth with higher defect density.

truth.properties.sample_s0.chemical.corrosion_: Oxide film thickness 16.2 ± 0.8 nm, composition mainly Fe and Al oxides with low Al content; high defect density (ND 0.70×10^21/cm3, NA 2.25×10^21/cm3); provides poor protection.
truth.properties.sample_s950.chemical.corrosio: Rapid formation of dense Al-rich oxide film (90.17 at% Al) with thickness 25.2 ± 1.2 nm, low defect density (ND 0.26×10^21/cm3, NA 0.68×10^21/cm3); protective efficiency 30.75%.
truth.properties.sample_s950.chemical.oxidatio: Al-rich oxide film (90.17 at% Al, 25.2 nm)
truth.properties.sample_s1050.chemical.corrosi: Oxide film thickness 21.6 ± 1 nm, moderate Al content, defect density ND 0.38×10^21/cm3, NA 1.03×10^21/cm3.
truth.properties.sample_s1150.chemical.corrosi: Oxide film thickness 18 ± 0.9 nm, reduced Al content (~69.91 at%), higher Fe/Mn content, defect density ND 0.49×10^21/cm3, NA 1.25×10^21/cm3; protective efficiency 17.95%.

prediction.properties.sample_s0.chemical.corrosion_: Poor; highest breakdown current density (9.92 μA/cm²) and lowest polarization resistance among all samples.
prediction.properties.sample_s0.chemical.oxidation_: Air-formed oxide film removed before polarization; native oxide film approximately 16.2 ± 0.8 nm with comparable Fe and Al content.
prediction.properties.sample_s950.chemical.corrosio: Excellent; lowest breakdown current density (2.72 μA/cm²), highest polarization resistance, and lowest defect density in oxide film.
prediction.properties.sample_s950.chemical.oxidatio: Dense Al-rich oxide film with thickness 25.2 ± 1.2 nm and Al content up to 90.17 at%.
prediction.properties.sample_s1050.chemical.oxidati: Oxide film thickness approximately 21.6 ± 1 nm; Al content high in outer film but Fe increases with depth.
prediction.properties.sample_s1150.chemical.corrosi: Poorer than S950; breakdown current density 8.84 μA/cm².
prediction.properties.sample_s1150.chemical.oxidati: Thinner oxide film (18 ± 0.9 nm) with reduced Al content (69.91 at%) and higher Fe/Mn content compared to S950.

truth.performance.sample_s950.comparative_perf: corrosion rate reduced by 43% compared to S1150

prediction.performance.sample_s950.comparative_perf: 43 % lower corrosion rate than coarse-grained S1150 (0.160 mm/y vs 0.281 mm/y)

truth.characterization_methods.idx_0.key_findi: Single-phase austenite (FCC) with peaks (111), (200), (220), (311); peak intensity changes after solution treatment.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_s0.key_f: High dislocation density; κ-carbides (5-10 nm) present; after heat treatment dislocations reduced, dislocation tangles at twin boundaries.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_s950.key: Uniform equiaxed grains after 950°C solution treatment.
truth.characterization_methods.idx_3.purpose: Grain size, grain boundary character, KAM analysis
truth.characterization_methods.idx_3.key_findi: Grain size increases from 8.7 to 32 μm; GB density decreases; S950 has highest ATB and HAGB density; KAM reduces from 1.62 (S0) to ~0.4.
truth.characterization_methods.idx_5.purpose: Surface oxide film composition and chemical states
truth.characterization_methods.idx_5.key_findi: Al content decreases with grain coarsening (S950: 90.17 at% Al; S1150: 69.91 at%); Fe, Mn oxides present in small amounts.
truth.characterization_methods.idx_6.technique: AES (ULVAC-PHI PHI-700, 5 kV, sputter rate 3.6 nm/min rel. SiO2)
truth.characterization_methods.idx_6.purpose: Oxide film thickness and depth composition profile
truth.characterization_methods.idx_6.key_findi: Film thickness: S0 16.2 nm, S950 25.2 nm, S1050 21.6 nm, S1150 18 nm; Al-rich oxide in S950 uniformly distributed.
truth.characterization_methods.idx_7.purpose: Surface potential mapping of grain boundaries
truth.characterization_methods.idx_7.key_findi: HAGBs show lower potential than grain interior; ATBs show higher potential; potential difference ~30 mV for ATBs.
truth.characterization_methods.idx_8.technique: Electrochemical testing: OCP, EIS, potentiodynamic polarization, CV, Mott-Schottky (Solartron Modulab XM)
truth.characterization_methods.idx_8.purpose: Corrosion resistance and oxide film semiconductor properties
truth.characterization_methods.idx_8.key_findi: i_bre order S0> S1150> S1050> S950; Rp highest for S950; film shows n-type and p-type behavior.

prediction.characterization_methods.sample_s0.techn: AES depth profiling (ULVAC-PHI PHI-700, 5 kV, 3.6 nm/min sputter rate)
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_s0.purpo: Elemental depth distribution in oxide film
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_s0.key_f: Oxide film thickness approximately 16.2 ± 0.8 nm; comparable Fe and Al content throughout film.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_s950.tec: AES depth profiling (ULVAC-PHI PHI-700, 5 kV, 3.6 nm/min sputter rate)
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_s950.pur: Elemental depth distribution in oxide film
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_s950.key: Thickest oxide film (25.2 ± 1.2 nm) with uniformly distributed Al oxides and low Fe/Mn content.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_s1050.te: AES depth profiling (ULVAC-PHI PHI-700, 5 kV, 3.6 nm/min sputter rate)
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_s1050.pu: Elemental depth distribution in oxide film
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_s1050.ke: Oxide film thickness approximately 21.6 ± 1 nm; Al content high in outer film but Fe increases with sputtering depth.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_s1150.te: AES depth profiling (ULVAC-PHI PHI-700, 5 kV, 3.6 nm/min sputter rate)
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_s1150.pu: Elemental depth distribution in oxide film
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_s1150.ke: Thinner oxide film (18 ± 0.9 nm); Al content decreases and Fe content increases with sputtering depth.

truth.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: Thermodynamic stability of Fe, Mn, Al oxides/hydroxides in Fe-Mn-Al-H2O system at 25°C
Calculated to confirm Al2O3 stable under experimental conditions, FeO and MnO stable regions limited.

prediction.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: E-pH diagram (Pourbaix diagram) for Fe-Mn-Al-H2O system
Predict stable oxidation/hydroxide products of Al, Fe, and Mn under experimental conditions
Calculated at 25 °C with Fe/Mn/Al activities of 1×10⁻¹⁰ M and 1 atm gas pressure; confirms Al forms stable Al2O3, Fe mainly as Fe²⁺, and Mn as Mn²⁺ under test conditions.
prediction.computational_details.idx_1.simulation_m: Equivalent circuit fitting of electrochemical impedance spectra
prediction.computational_details.idx_1.validation_a: Excellent agreement between experimental EIS data and fitted curves for all samples; two time constants observed.
prediction.computational_details.idx_1.analytical_m: Rs-(R1Q1)-(R2Q2) equivalent circuit with constant phase elements
Model nonideal capacitive response of oxide film and solution/film interface
Rs (solution resistance), R1 and Q1 (diffusion resistance and CPE at solution/film interface), R2 and Q2 (oxide film resistance and CPE), n1 and n2 (dispersion indices)
CPE impedance Z_CPE = 1/[Q(jω)^n] used to account for surface porosity and inhomogeneity; polarization resistance R_p = R1 + R2.
prediction.computational_details.idx_2.analytical_m: Describe evolution of average grain size with solution treatment temperature and time
D0 (initial grain size), D (final grain size), k (diffusion coefficient), k0 (material constant), Q (activation energy for diffusion), R (gas constant), T (absolute temperature), t (time)
D² - D0² = k t, where k = k0 exp(-Q/RT); explains accelerated grain growth and reduced GB density at higher temperatures due to increased atomic mobility and GB migration.

truth.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Table 1 summarizes literature on corrosion of LDSs with various alloying additions (Cr, Mo, Ni, Cu) and their effects on passive film and pitting resistance.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Figure 4 grain size distribution data show heterogeneous distribution for S0 and uniform distributions for S950–S1150; exact average grain size of S1050 not numerically given but lies between 8.7 and 32 μm.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: KAM value decreases from 1.62 (S0) to ~0.4 after solution treatment (Fig. 4e).
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: EIS equivalent circuit fitting parameters (n1, n2, Q1, Q2) are given in Tables 2 and 3 for all samples.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: Cyclic voltammetry shows Al oxidation peak at −0.81 V_SCE and Fe oxidation peak at −0.56 V_SCE; integrated charge density for Al peak in first cycle indicates more complete Al oxidation for S950.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: The Mott-Schottky analysis carrier densities ND and NA are shown in Fig. 12b.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: The protective efficiency (PE) calculated from oxide film resistance is 30.75% for S950 and 17.95% for S1150.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: Grain growth model Eq. (8)-(9) used to describe grain size evolution with temperature.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_8.value: Oxide film formation mechanism: preferential dissolution of Al at grain boundaries, high GB density promotes uniform and rapid film growth.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_9.value: Figure 20 schematics illustrate the difference in oxide film formation and corrosion mechanisms between fine- and coarse-grained structures.

prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: The fine-grained sample S950 forms an Al-rich oxide film with thickness 25.2 ± 1.2 nm and the lowest defect densities (acceptor density N_A 0.68×10²¹/cm³ and donor density N_D 0.26×10²¹/cm³), while the coarse-grained S1150 film thickness is 18 ± 0.9 nm with reduced Al content (from 90.17 at% to 69.91 at%) and higher defect density.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Potentiodynamic polarization breakdown current densities (i_bre) in 3.5 wt% NaCl solution: S0 9.92 μA/cm², S950 2.72 μA/cm², S1050 6.55 μA/cm², S1150 8.84 μA/cm².
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: EIS polarization resistance (R_p) of as-polished samples: S0 6036 Ω·cm², S950 12486 Ω·cm², S1050 8014 Ω·cm², S1150 6992 Ω·cm²; after 7200 s potentiostatic polarization: S0 6143, S950 21355, S1050 14144, S1150 10756 Ω·cm².
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: Oxide film protection efficiency (PE) calculated from R2 values after potentiostatic polarization: S950 30.75 %, S1150 17.95 %.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: Mott-Schottky analysis shows duplex semiconductor behavior (n-type in the potential region -1.13 to -0.93 V_SCE and p-type in -0.93 to -0.67 V_SCE) for all samples; carrier densities increase with grain size: S950 N_D 0.26×10²¹/cm³ and N_A 0.68×10²¹/cm³; S1150 N_D 0.49×10²¹/cm³ and N_A 1.25×10²¹/cm³.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: Cyclic voltammetry reveals an Al oxidation peak at approximately -0.81 V_SCE and an Fe oxidation peak at approximately -0.56 V_SCE; S950 exhibits the highest Al oxidation charge density in the first cycle (2.55×10⁻⁵ C/cm²), which suppresses the Fe oxidation peak in the first cycle.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: During immersion testing (0.5–168 h) in 3.5 wt% NaCl, EIS R_p of S950 reaches 114.9 kΩ·cm² at 168 h, significantly higher than S1150 (50.6 kΩ·cm²), indicating rapid formation of a dense oxide film on fine-grained steel.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: XPS valence state analysis of oxide films: Fe exists as Fe²⁺ (709.8 eV), Fe³⁺ (711.5 eV), and Fe_hy³⁺ (713.3 eV); Mn as Mn²⁺ (640.4 eV), Mn⁴⁺ (641.8 eV), and satellite peak (646.2 eV) except in S950 where only Mn²⁺ is detected; Al as Al³⁺ (74.1 eV); O as OH⁻ (531.6 eV), H₂O (533.2 eV), and O²⁻ (529.7 eV).
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_8.value: SKPFM surface potential measurements show HAGBs have the lowest potential (most active anodic dissolution sites), while ATBs exhibit a higher potential than the matrix with an approximately 30 mV potential difference that drives microgalvanic coupling and localized dissolution along twin boundaries.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_9.value: The corrosion mechanism schematic indicates that fine-grained structures (S950) with high GB density facilitate preferential Al dissolution at grain boundaries, yielding a continuous, dense Al-rich oxide film, whereas coarse-grained structures (S1150) with sparse boundaries develop thinner, discontinuous oxide films with higher Fe/Mn content and defect channels.
